mnemonics: CENTS: Central fact table, Extended dimensions, Numeric facts, Time-variant, Surrogate keys
keyPoints: A star schema consists of a central fact table connected to multiple dimension tables.
The fact table contains quantitative data (measures) about a business process.
Dimension tables contain descriptive attributes (context) related to the fact data.
commonMistakes: Creating overly complex, normalized dimension tables (snowflaking) when a simple star would suffice.
Placing textual or descriptive information in the fact table instead of in dimension tables.
Forgetting to include a time dimension table, which is crucial for almost all fact tables.
